title: frangula purshiana
intro: Frangula purshiana, also known as Cascara Sagrada, is a flowering plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include laxative and anti-inflammatory effects due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Frangula purshiana include relief from constipation, improved digestion, and reduced symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Frangula purshiana can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or extract, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote regular bowel movements and overall gastrointestinal health.
